mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show a statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent labor market contraction that reduces consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 55% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation decelerates while the federal funds rate remains stable, real rates decline, improving equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or null predictive IC, or positive returns do not materialize when CPIAUCSL growth falls below 2% while FEDFUNDS is unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in > 60% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing and utility output that boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently weak nonfarm payrolls growth signals labor market slack that dampens wage pressures and consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth falls below 100k for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: The threshold condition triggers but SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 70% of cases, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear periods with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises, the economy enters a stagflationary or recessionary phase that compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive forward returns in > 55% of triggered quarters, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises borrowing costs and equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 58% in high-inflation regimes.
